title: The incredible dolphin
content:
Many people say dolphins are intelligent. They seem to be able to think, understand, and learn things quickly. But are they as smart as humans, or are they more like cats and dogs? Dolphins use their brains quite differently from the way people do. But scientists say dolphins and humans are alike in some ways. How?

Communication

Like humans, every dolphin has its own "name." The name is a special whistle. Each dolphin chooses a whistle for itself, usually by its first birthday. Dolphins are like people in other ways, too. They "talk" to each other about a lot of things-such as their age, their feelings, and possible danger. They also use a system of sounds and body language to communicate. Understanding dolphin conversation is not easy for humans. No one "speaks dolphin" yet, but some scientists are trying to learn.

Play

Dolphins live in groups called pods, and they often join other dolphins from different pods to play games and have fun-just like people. Sometimes they chase other dolphins carrying objects (e.g., seaweed) and throw these objects back and forth. Scientists believe playing together is something only intelligent animals do.

Teamwork

Dolphins and humans are similar in another way: They both make plans for getting things they want. In the seas of southern Brazil, for example, dolphins use an intelligent method to get food. When there are fish near a boat, dolphins signal to the fishermen to put their nets in the water. With the dolphins' help, the men can catch a lot of fish. Why do dolphins assist the men? There is an advantage for the dolphins: They get to eat some of the fish that escape from the net.
content_html:
Many people say dolphins are intelligent. They seem to be able to think, understand, and learn things quickly. But are they as smart as humans, or are they more like cats and dogs? Dolphins use their brains quite differently from the way people do. But scientists say dolphins and humans are alike in some ways. How?

Communication

Like humans, every dolphin has its own "name." The name is a special whistle. Each dolphin chooses a whistle for itself, usually by its first birthday. Dolphins are like people in other ways, too. They "talk" to each other about a lot of things-such as their age, their feelings, and possible danger. They also use a system of sounds and body language to communicate. Understanding dolphin conversation is not easy for humans. No one "speaks dolphin" yet, but some scientists are trying to learn.

Play

Dolphins live in groups called pods, and they often join other dolphins from different pods to play games and have fun-just like people. Sometimes they chase other dolphins carrying objects (e.g., seaweed) and throw these objects back and forth. Scientists believe playing together is something only intelligent animals do.

Teamwork

Dolphins and humans are similar in another way: They both make plans for getting things they want. In the seas of southern Brazil, for example, dolphins use an intelligent method to get food. When there are fish near a boat, dolphins signal to the fishermen to put their nets in the water. With the dolphins' help, the men can catch a lot of fish. Why do dolphins assist the men? There is an advantage for the dolphins: They get to eat some of the fish that escape from the net.

questions.question_text: What does the reading NOT mention?

options.text: how dolphins communicate with each other
how dolphins play games and have fun
how dolphins work together to get food
how dolphins move quickly through the water

questions.explanation: Bài đọc đề cập đến cách giao tiếp, vui chơi và làm việc nhóm của cá heo, nhưng không nói về tốc độ bơi của chúng.

questions.question_text: The author mentions cats and dogs in paragraph A to show that

options.text: cats and dogs are as intelligent as humans
scientists have studied the brains of cats and dogs
there are different levels of intelligence
dolphins prefer playing with cats and dogs

questions.explanation: Tác giả đặt câu hỏi liệu cá heo thông minh như con người hay ở mức độ giống như chó mèo để so sánh các cấp độ thông minh khác nhau.

questions.question_text: Where does a dolphin get its "name"?

options.text: It chooses it for itself.
It gets it from its mother.
It gets it from scientists.
It is given by other members of the pod.

questions.explanation: Bài đọc nêu rõ: 'Each dolphin chooses a whistle for itself' (Mỗi con cá heo tự chọn một tiếng huýt sáo cho chính mình).

questions.question_text: Which sentence about dolphin language is true?

options.text: Dolphin conversation is easy for humans to understand.
Dolphins "talk" to each other about many things.
Dolphins can't understand dolphins from other pods.
Dolphins only use body language to communicate.

questions.explanation: Bài đọc xác nhận cá heo 'nói chuyện' với nhau về nhiều thứ như tuổi tác, cảm xúc và nguy hiểm.

questions.question_text: Why do dolphins sometimes help fishermen?

options.text: The dolphins can get food that way.
Dolphins are naturally kind to all humans.
The fishermen ask the dolphins for help verbally.
Dolphins enjoy being near fishing boats for fun.

questions.explanation: Cá heo giúp ngư dân vì có lợi ích: chúng được ăn những con cá thoát ra khỏi lưới.
